steps.title: Separate fixed and recurring costs
steps.content: The 35-dollar activation fee is paid only once, so it is a constant. The 23-dollar monthly cost is multiplied by the number of months $t$, contributing $23t$.

steps.title: Combine into a total-cost equation
steps.content: Total cost is fixed plus recurring: $c = 23t + 35$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $c=\frac{t}{23}+35$ — would mean the cost per month decreases as $t$ grows, not a flat 23 dollars per month. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $c=\frac{t}{35}+23$ — 23 dollars becomes the constant and 35 dollars becomes a divisor. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $c=23t+35$ — correctly adds 23 dollars per month to the one-time 35-dollar fee. Matches.
  • Choice D: $c=35t+23$ — swaps the monthly rate and the activation fee. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — $c=23t+35$.
